nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: The ground shudders. Stone tears itself from the arena floor and rises — a towering figure, part creature, part living rock, veins of dim crystal light pulsing beneath its surface. Its eyes open slowly, burning a deep amber. "Another one. Another human crawling across what remains of this land."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "I am Varek. Born from the earth of the Verge. Shaped by Malakor's will." The creature steps forward, the stone floor fracturing under its weight. "She is my mother. And you have come here to steal from her. Again."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I'm not here to steal anything. I'm here to end this."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 7] Save the speech. Every creature on this island gave me one. Step aside or get broken."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 7] Malakor shaped you from the earth and bound you to this arena. You are not a soldier. You are a lock."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] You... came out of the floor. Can you go back into the floor? I'm asking politely."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Weak] There has clearly been some sort of misunderstanding. I was just... admiring the stonework."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "End this." Varek repeats the words slowly, as if tasting them. "Your kind said the same thing twenty years ago. They came with torches and promises. They left with the crystal veins ripped from the earth, and the blood of Malakor's kin soaked deep into the ground."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Varek tilts its massive head. "Bold. Jade, is that your name? Malakor spoke of you. She said you would be exactly this foolish."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Something shifts in Varek's expression — not surprise, but recognition. "Clever. Yes. She shaped me for this purpose and no other. I am the last gate before the Heart Crystal." Its eyes narrow. "And you will not pass it."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Varek stares at you for a long, silent moment. "Malakor told me the Herald would be a great danger to the Verge." A low, grinding pause. "She did not mention you would be... this."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "She felt the first crystal shatter. Across the sea, in the dark of this arena, I felt her pain." Varek's fists close, the crystal veins flaring bright. "And now you come for the second one. She knows. I know. You will not leave this arena with it."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Then you already know how this ends. Move, Varek."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] I was sent here by people who lied to me. But the crystal still has to fall. Don't make me go through you."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 7] I beat everything else on this island. You're just the last thing in my way."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] Which crystal specifically? There were a lot of glowy rocks and I may have bumped a few by accident already."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Move." Varek repeats the word like it means nothing. "My mother built this world from broken rock and her own suffering. I have waited in the dark of this arena for twenty years, knowing your kind would return." The ground heaves. "I will not move."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "You were deceived." Varek is still for a moment. "That is not nothing. But it changes nothing either. Whether you are a pawn or a king, the crystal does not fall while I still stand."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Varek turns its gaze toward you very slowly. "By accident." A long, grinding pause. "You destroyed Malakor's creation. By accident."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Malakor called you the Herald of Eirene. She feared what you might become." Varek drops into a fighting stance, the arena walls beginning to glow with amber light. "I am not Malakor. I do not fear you. I know only my purpose."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Then we do this the hard way."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 7] Perfect. I prefer it that way."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Weak] I hate this island so much."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Varek kneels in the rubble of the arena. Cracks run across its stone body. The crystal veins are dark and fading. Its voice comes out low and broken, like something deep underground finally giving way.

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "So. You are as strong as she feared." Varek looks past you toward the Heart Crystal. "I held this arena for twenty years. I thought... twenty years was enough time."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I'm sorry. I didn't want this."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] You protected this place faithfully. But the crystal still has to fall."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 7] You fought well, Varek. Better than anything else I've faced."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] Are you... going to be okay? You look like a wall that got hit by a bigger wall."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Sorry." The word seems foreign to Varek. "Malakor told me humans use that word often. That it costs them nothing." A crack spreads across its chest. "I believe you mean it. It does not change what comes next."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "You sound like someone who has already decided." The cracks in Varek's body deepen. "A human who thinks. That is rarer than I expected. I wonder if Malakor knew that about you."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "You are strong. Yes." Varek looks at its shattered hands. "The humans who came twenty years ago were a mob. Greedy and loud and many. You are something else entirely."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Varek stares at you for a long moment. "You are a very strange creature, Jade." The grinding of its crumbling body almost sounds like a laugh. "Very strange."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Before you take the crystal..." Varek's voice is quieter now, the amber glow nearly gone from its eyes. "Malakor. She is not what your people told you she is. She did not come for your world. Your world came for hers."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "When the last crystal falls... go to her. Not as a destroyer. Go to her and listen. Whatever she tells you..." Stone crumbles from Varek's shoulders, dust settling around your feet. "...listen. That is all I ask."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I'll listen. I promise."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] You served Malakor to your last breath. I won't forget that."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] I... yeah. I will. I don't always listen great, but I'll try this time."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Good." The last of Varek breaks apart, stone dust rising slowly in the amber light. The Heart Crystal pulses once — bright — then dims to a low, patient hum.
